meta.analysis_method: Memory diffing via AltirraBridge + disassembly analysis
notes: Game bypasses Atari OS almost entirely: takes over ANTIC, DLI, VBI, POKEY.
Display uses ANTIC mode $0E (160-pixel 4-color) with DLI-based kernel at $B500.
The disassembler shows OS symbolic names for zero-page (e.g. DOSINI, ICSTAZ) -- these are NOT OS usage; the game repurposes those bytes as game variables.

$00.name: sprite_gfx_ptr_lo
$00.purpose: Low byte of pointer to current sprite graphics data for DLI rendering
$00.evidence: Used in DLI kernel at $B590 as indirect pointer base: lda ($00),Y

$01.name: sprite_gfx_ptr_hi
$01.purpose: High byte of sprite graphics pointer
$01.evidence: Paired with $00 for indirect addressing in DLI

$02.name: sprite2_gfx_ptr_lo
$02.purpose: Low byte of second sprite graphics pointer
$02.evidence: Used at $B5B2 with lda ($02),Y for second entity rendering

$04.name: scanline_x_pos
$04.purpose: Current entity X position during DLI scanline rendering
$04.evidence: Set at $B575 from computed entity X, used for horizontal positioning

$05.name: scroll_position_fine
$05.purpose: Fine vertical scroll position (increments +12 per 60-frame test, i.e. +1 per ~5 frames)
$05.evidence: Always changes in all tests with consistent positive delta proportional to frame count; linked to $C2 which tracks identically

$06.name: dli_scanline_counter
$06.purpose: DLI scanline counter - tracks which scan line of the playfield we're rendering
$06.evidence: Used at $B562/$B565 in DLI kernel: ldy $06; iny; sty $06

$07.name: entity_flags_temp
$07.purpose: Temporary storage for entity attribute flags during DLI rendering
$07.evidence: Loaded from entity table $42,Y at $B584, tested with BIT at $B5D2

$09.name: current_sector
$09.purpose: Current river sector/zone index (0-based, increments as terrain changes)
$09.evidence: Widely referenced: $AF80 tests bit 0 for bank selection, $B06E uses ($09 AND $0E)*2+$80 as screen buffer page, $B247 tests bit 0 for double-buffering swap

$0A.name: entity_scan_temp
$0A.purpose: Temporary for DLI entity scanning loop
$0A.evidence: Used in DLI at $B660: stx $0A, later restored for entity count tracking

$0B.name: color_pf1_temp
$0B.purpose: Temporary color register value for playfield 1 during DLI
$0B.evidence: At $B5F4: lda $0B; sta COLPF1. Set from entity rendering

$0C-$14.name: scanline_row_table
$0C-$14.purpose: Array of 9 scanline row counters for playfield rendering - each byte tracks the vertical position within a playfield row
$0C-$14.evidence: All 9 bytes change together with identical deltas (+12 per 60 frames). Initialized from $BAE1 table at $B0F1. DLI uses them at $B56B: cmp $0C,Y to determine when entities appear on screen

$15.name: scroll_row_bottom
$15.purpose: Bottom-most visible row scroll counter (slightly different rate from $0C-$14)
$15.evidence: Changes in all tests but with larger initial delta (+44 vs +12 in 'up' test) suggesting it tracks the bottom edge

$16.name: scroll_row_top
$16.purpose: Top-most visible row scroll counter, offset from $05
$16.evidence: Always changes with $05-like delta (+12/frame-batch), offset by a constant from $05

$17.name: init_flag
$17.purpose: Initialization complete flag (set to $FF at init end)
$17.evidence: Set at $B0F9 after init loop: stx $17 (with X=$FF after decrementing past 0)

$18.name: distance_counter
$18.purpose: Distance/frame counter - increments steadily as the game progresses (half rate of $05)
$18.evidence: Always increases: +6 per 60 frames (half the delta of $05). Used in scoring/distance tracking

$19.name: random_seed_or_timer
$19.purpose: Pseudo-random or timing value that fluctuates each test
$19.evidence: Changes in most tests with irregular deltas (-1, -3, +4, -8, +6), suggesting randomness or timing-dependent state

$1A-$22.name: collision_register_shadows
$1A-$22.purpose: Shadow copies of GTIA collision registers, refreshed each DLI frame
$1A-$22.evidence: DLI at $B510-$B525 sets $1A-$22 to $FF. Later, conditional stores from hardware collision registers at $B5E0-$B720 clear individual bits. Post-frame code tests these for hit detection

$23.name: game_state_flags
$23.purpose: Bit-packed game state: bit 2 = bridge zone flag, bit 7 = scroll direction or death state
$23.evidence: Tested at $AFD0 (AND #$04, EOR #$D6 for color), at $B1C8 (AND #$04 for sector boundary), at $B1D0 (BIT = BMI for bit 7)

$24.name: sound_state
$24.evidence: Tested at $B2BA: lda $24; cmp #$02; bcc $B2CE to determine sound channel routing

$26.name: terrain_gen_counter_lo
$26.purpose: Low byte of terrain generation position counter
$26.evidence: Used in VBI at $B450+ for terrain scrolling: ldy $26; dey; loaded/stored in terrain generation logic

$27.name: terrain_gen_position
$27.purpose: Terrain generation write position into screen buffer
$27.evidence: At $B470+: sta $27; used at $B750: lda $27; sta HPOSP3 for river bank positioning

$29.name: game_active_flag
$29.purpose: Game active/paused flag. Bit 7 = game over or paused state
$29.evidence: Tested at $B086: BIT $29; BMI $B0C6 to skip score rendering when game not active

$2C.name: enemy_spawn_timer
$2C.purpose: Timer for enemy/obstacle spawning - changes intermittently
$2C.evidence: Changes only in 'up' and 'idle' tests with irregular values, suggesting event-triggered updates

$2E.name: sector_boundary_flag
$2E.purpose: Non-zero when at a sector boundary, triggers terrain recalculation
$2E.evidence: Tested at $B1CC: lda $2E; beq $B1D9 to control sector transition behavior

$2F.name: scroll_sub_position
$2F.purpose: Sub-pixel scroll position - wraps around irregularly
$2F.evidence: Changes every test with non-monotonic pattern, consistent with fine scroll tracking

$31.name: engine_sound_counter
$31.evidence: At $B41C: lda $31; beq skip; dec $31; lsr; bcc skip; changes engine sound register

$32.name: terrain_offset
$32.purpose: Current terrain rendering offset within sector
$32.evidence: Changes every test with mixed positive/negative deltas, consistent with terrain position tracking

$34-$36.name: bullet_state
$34-$36.evidence: $35 goes $00->$FF on 'up' (first shot fires), back to $00 on 'idle' (bullet expired). $34/$36 change with bullet lifecycle

$39.name: player2_hpos
$39.purpose: Horizontal position for player 1 (second player sprite, used for bullet or dual-purpose)

$3A.name: distance_counter_2
$3A.purpose: Second distance counter, tracks identically to $40 - possibly display-related copy
$3A.evidence: Always changes with exact same delta as $40 in every test

$3C.name: player0_hpos
$3C.purpose: Horizontal position for player 0 sprite (the jet)

$40.name: display_scroll_counter
$40.purpose: Display scroll counter - mirrors $3A, used for screen update timing
$40.evidence: Identical deltas to $3A in every test, always increasing

$42-$4B.name: entity_attribute_array
$42-$4B.purpose: Array of entity attribute bytes (type/state flags) indexed by entity slot
$42-$4B.evidence: DLI reads at $B584: lda $42,Y for entity flags. Individual slots change when entities spawn ($43=$40 in idle, $47=$40 on right, $49=$40 on fire, etc.)

$4C.name: prev_game_state
$4C.purpose: Previous frame's game state flags - compared with $23 for edge detection
$4C.evidence: At $B1C4: lda $4C; eor $23; and #$04 to detect sector boundary transitions

$4D.name: bridge_countdown
$4D.purpose: Bridge/checkpoint countdown - decrements as bridges are passed or destroyed
$4D.evidence: Decrements by 1 in 'up', 'idle', 'down' tests (every ~120 frames), consistent with bridge spacing

$4E.name: bridge_counter_2
$4E.purpose: Secondary bridge counter, tracks similar to $4D with slight offset
$4E.evidence: Changes in parallel with $4D but also affected by firing, suggesting bridge-related game logic

$4F.name: terrain_bank_select
$4F.purpose: Terrain data bank selector (0 or non-zero) for double-buffered terrain
$4F.evidence: Tested at $AFF4: lda $4F; bne $AFEC and at $B034: ldy $4F; beq $B03B for conditional terrain offset

$52.name: player_x_position
$52.purpose: Player jet horizontal position in game coordinates (range ~$14-$2C, center ~$20)
$52.evidence: Memory diffing confirms: decreases when moving left, increases when moving right. Changes slowly (1 unit per several frames)

$53.name: fuel_bar_left_edge
$53.purpose: Left edge X coordinate of the fuel bar display
$53.evidence: Increases steadily as fuel is consumed (bar shrinks from left), correlates with distance traveled

$54.name: fuel_bar_right_edge
$54.purpose: Right edge X coordinate of the fuel bar display
$54.evidence: Decreases as fuel depletes (bar shrinks from right), anti-correlates with $53

$56.name: lives_remaining
$56.purpose: Number of lives remaining (1-based: 1 = last life)
$56.evidence: Used at $B223: ldx $56; dex; beq return, and in score display logic at $B22E to control high-score table writes

$57.name: player_sprite_hpos
$57.purpose: Horizontal position register value for player sprite (written to HPOSP2 in DLI)

$5A.name: scroll_direction
$5A.purpose: Vertical scroll direction flag - toggles between 1 and 2
$5A.evidence: Alternates: 1->2 on 'up', 2->1 on 'idle', 1->2 on 'down'. Controls terrain scroll direction

$5B.name: missile_hpos_base
$5B.purpose: Base horizontal position for missiles (shifted right 3 and offset to $5C for HPOSM1)
$5B.evidence: DLI at $B527: lda $5B; lsr; lsr; lsr; clc; adc #$5C; sta HPOSM1

$5D.name: lives_display
$5D.purpose: Lives display value (decrements as lives are lost, format: $11=initial, $10, $0F...)
$5D.evidence: Memory diffing shows slow decrement (-1 per ~120 frames in some tests), consistent with display countdown

$5E.name: scanline_count
$5E.purpose: Number of scanlines to render per terrain row (set to 6 at $B1FA)
$5E.evidence: At $B1F8: lda #$06; sta $5E. Controls terrain row height in rendering

$5F.name: difficulty_level
$5F.purpose: Current difficulty level or game phase indicator
$5F.evidence: Decreases infrequently ($0D->$07 in 'up' test), consistent with difficulty ramping

$60.name: terrain_read_index
$60.purpose: Circular buffer read index for terrain data (range $01-$50, wraps)
$60.evidence: At $B194: ldx $60; cpx #$50; bcc ok; ldx #$4E. Incremented at $B1BD: inx; stx $60

$66-$69.name: terrain_seed_current
$66-$69.purpose: Current terrain generation LFSR seed (4 bytes). Copied to/from $AA-$AC random state
$66-$69.evidence: At $B194-$B1BA: saved to $AD-$B0, then $AA-$AC copied in. At $B1E8-$B1F6: restored from $AD-$B0. Part of terrain PRNG system

$6B-$74.name: terrain_seed_alternate
$6B-$74.purpose: Alternate terrain generation seed for double-buffered terrain (mirrors $60-$69 structure)
$6B-$74.evidence: At $B247 swap routine: exchanges $60-$6A with $6B-$74 when sector bit toggles

$76.name: fuel_level
$76.purpose: Current fuel level (decrements steadily; 0 = empty = game over)
$76.evidence: Monotonically decreasing in every test: -6 per 60 frames. Checked at $B696: lda $76; beq (fuel empty). Central game mechanic

$77.name: double_buffer_flag
$77.purpose: Double-buffer bank flag (0 or 1) - swapped each sector by $B247
$77.evidence: At $B247: lda $09; and #$01; eor $77; sta $77. DLI at $B544: ldx $77; bne for color selection

$79.name: explosion_timer
$79.evidence: Tested at $B690: ldy $79; beq skip for explosion color handling

$7A.name: sound_param_a
$7A.purpose: Sound parameter A passed to VBI sound routine
$7A.evidence: At $B1C2: sta $7A, then read in VBI at $B2D2: ldx $7A; beq skip for sound frequency control

$7B.name: sound_param_b
$7B.purpose: Sound parameter B for sound routine pitch/channel selection
$7B.evidence: At $B1C0: sty $7B, then read at $B2D0: lda $7B for sound generation

$80-$8F.name: checksum_table
$80-$8F.purpose: Copy-protection checksum table (16 bytes) - initialized at boot, verified by $B298
$80-$8F.evidence: At $A037: jsr $B298 which sums $80-$8E with EOR $A0. Boot code at $A03A compares against $0090,Y

$90-$9F.name: checksum_expected
$90-$9F.purpose: Expected checksum values for copy protection verification
$90-$9F.evidence: Written at $B291: sta $0090,Y from $B298 computation during game init

$A0.name: checksum_xor_key
$A0.evidence: At $B29F: eor $A0 inside the checksum accumulation loop

$A1-$A2.name: source_ptr
$A1-$A2.purpose: General-purpose source pointer for terrain data / graphics copy operations
$A1-$A2.evidence: Used throughout rendering: at $B012 lda ($A1),Y for terrain source; at $B261 sta $A1 for score comparison. $A2 used as high byte or page counter

$A3-$A4.name: screen_ptr
$A3-$A4.purpose: Screen buffer destination pointer (points into $2000-$3F00 screen RAM area)
$A3-$A4.evidence: Used at $AFFF: sta ($A3),Y for screen writes; at $B04D: adc $A3 to advance through screen buffer. $A4 steadily decreases as screen fills bottom-to-top

$A5.name: score_write_index
$A5.purpose: Write index into score display area at $3F04
$A5.evidence: At $B0D8: ldx $A5; then sta $3F04,X; inc $A5. Steadily increases as score digits are written

$A6.name: distance_write_index
$A6.purpose: Write index for distance/bridge counter display at $3F04
$A6.evidence: At $B0C7: ldx $A6; then sta $3F04,X; inc $A6. Parallel to $A5 for different display field

$AA-$AC.name: prng_state
$AA-$AC.evidence: At $B183: shifts/XORs $AB, rotates into $AA/$AC/$AB - classic LFSR. Highly volatile values across all tests confirm randomness

$AD-$B0.name: terrain_seed_save
$AD-$B0.purpose: Save area for terrain seed during sector transitions
$AD-$B0.evidence: At $B19E-$B1AA: $66-$69 saved to $AD-$B0. At $B1E8-$B1F6: restored from $AD-$B0

$B1.name: sound_frequency
$B1.purpose: Sound frequency/pitch value for POKEY audio register writes
$B1.evidence: At $B05C: lda #$90; sta $B1 (explosion sound). At $B082: sta $B1 for various sound effects. Steadily changes reflecting audio pitch tracking

$B2.name: digit_render_count
$B2.purpose: Counter for number of score digits to render
$B2.evidence: At $B08A: sta $B2; then $B0A3: dec $B2; bne loop

$B3.name: score_overflow
$B3.evidence: Changes only in 'idle' test ($00->$07), suggesting periodic event like distance bonus

$B4.name: screen_row_offset
$B4.purpose: Accumulated screen row byte offset for display list rendering
$B4.evidence: At $B057: adc $B4; sta $B4 - accumulates the number of bytes per rendered row

$B6.name: terrain_row_height
$B6.purpose: Current terrain row height in scanlines (saved/restored during terrain fill)
$B6.evidence: At $AFFA: sty $B6 (save Y), at $B004: lda $B6 (restore). Also decreases over time suggesting terrain parameter

$B7.name: fuel_burn_rate_or_position
$B7.purpose: Fuel consumption rate or fuel bar screen position (decreases over time)
$B7.evidence: Decreases in most tests, more slowly than $76. Pattern suggests derived fuel display metric

$B9.name: random_terrain_param
$B9.purpose: Random terrain parameter - cycles through values for river width/shape
$B9.evidence: Changes in most tests with large irregular deltas, consistent with terrain randomization

$BA-$BC.name: terrain_data_ptr
$BA-$BC.purpose: $BA/$BB = pointer to terrain shape data, $BC = byte count per row
$BA-$BC.evidence: At $B01A: ldy $BC; dey; lda ($BA),Y copies terrain data. At $B024-$B02D: $BA += $BC to advance

$BD-$BE.name: terrain_data_extra
$BD-$BE.evidence: Change only in 'up' test, suggesting they're set once during terrain init

$BF.name: vbi_skip_flag
$BF.purpose: Flag to skip portions of VBI terrain update
$BF.evidence: At $B740: lda $BF; bne skip - controls whether VBI processes terrain

$C2.name: master_scroll_counter
$C2.purpose: Master vertical scroll counter - identical to $05, possibly the primary copy
$C2.evidence: Changes in perfect lockstep with $05 in every test with identical deltas

$C3.name: scroll_direction_signed
$C3.purpose: Signed scroll direction indicator (+1 = down, -1/$FF = up)
$C3.evidence: Goes $00->$FF on up, $FF->$01 on idle (down), $01->$FF on down. Classic signed direction variable

$C4-$C5.name: terrain_init_params
$C4-$C5.purpose: Terrain initialization parameters set during first scroll
$C4-$C5.evidence: Change only once (in 'up' test), then stable. Set during initial terrain setup

$C6.name: bullet_sound_timer
$C6.purpose: Countdown timer for bullet firing sound effect
$C6.evidence: VBI at $B2A6: lda $C6; beq skip; dec $C6; then writes POKEY AUDF1/AUDC1

$C7.name: init_phase
$C7.evidence: Set to 0 at $B101, incremented at $B121 during init. VBI at $B430: lda $C7; bne skip

$C8-$CA.name: fuel_display_params
$C8-$CA.purpose: Fuel bar display parameters (positions/colors for the fuel gauge rendering)
$C8-$CA.evidence: $C8/$C9 change together with $53/$54 (fuel bar edges). $CA changes intermittently, possibly fuel color threshold

$0500-$050A.name: entity_slot_0
$0500-$050A.purpose: Entity table slot 0 (11 bytes) - properties for first enemy/object
$0500-$050A.fields: Bytes 7-8 appear to be Y/screen position, byte 9 = type/identifier, byte 10 = flags. First 7 bytes are often zero for inactive entities

$052C-$0536.name: entity_slot_4
$052C-$0536.purpose: Entity table slot 4 (5th and final entity slot)

$0600-$06BF.name: pm_player0_graphics
$0600-$06BF.purpose: Player/Missile graphics area for Player 0 (the jet sprite)

$0700-$07CF.name: pm_player1_graphics
$0700-$07CF.purpose: Player/Missile graphics for Player 1 (bullet or enemy)

$0B01-$0B08.name: high_score_table
$0B01-$0B08.purpose: High score table entries (8 bytes), indexed by life/game number

$1000-$103C.name: score_display_buffer
$1000-$103C.purpose: Screen buffer for score/status display line (written by $B07C+ digit renderer)

$2000-$3EFF.name: playfield_screen_ram
$2000-$3EFF.purpose: Double-buffered playfield graphics (ANTIC mode $0E, 40 bytes/row, ~96 rows x 2 banks)

$3F00-$3FB7.name: display_list
$3F00-$3FB7.purpose: ANTIC display list: 8 blank + DLI + 4 blank + mode $0E rows with LMS at $28A0 and $3000. JVB back to $3F00

$3F04-$3FA9.name: display_list_score_area
$3F04-$3FA9.purpose: Score/bridge counter display area embedded in display list (written by $B0C7/$B0D8)

$AF5B.name: render_fuel_bar
$AF5B.purpose: Renders the fuel bar gauge to screen memory at $1033. Uses $6A (fuel level) to determine bar length, fills with $9C (fuel color) then $00 (empty) up to 9 bytes

$AF80.name: setup_entity_rendering
$AF80.purpose: Sets up rendering parameters for current sector's entities. Tests $09 bit 0 to select between two sprite sets, loads entity shape pointers from $B494 table into $66-$69 and $60/$6B

$AFD0.name: update_bridge_color
$AFD0.purpose: Computes color for bridge/checkpoint zones. XORs $23 flags with $D6, checks entity count ($4E) and position ($0C+X) against threshold $5C to modify color

$AFEC.name: fill_terrain_solid
$AFEC.purpose: Fills a terrain row with solid pattern $AA (alternating bits = solid terrain). Entry point for solid riverbank fill

$AFF0.name: fill_terrain_full
$AFF0.purpose: Fills terrain row with $FF pattern (fully solid). Used for bridge/obstacle sections

$AFF4.name: fill_terrain_conditional
$AFF4.purpose: Conditionally fills terrain - checks $4F bank flag: if set uses $AA (solid), else $55 (half-density). Auto-selects terrain density

$B00F.name: copy_terrain_from_source
$B00F.purpose: Copies terrain shape data from source pointer ($A1) to screen buffer ($A3). Y bytes copied in reverse order

$B01A.name: copy_terrain_multi_row
$B01A.purpose: Copies multiple terrain rows from pointer ($BA/$BB) with $BC bytes per row. Advances source pointer after each row copy, then calls $B04B to advance screen pointer

$B034.name: copy_terrain_2byte_banked
$B034.purpose: Copies 2-byte terrain tile from source ($A1) to screen ($A3), with bank offset ($4F non-zero adds $10 to source address)

$B03B.name: copy_terrain_2byte
$B03B.purpose: Copies exactly 2 bytes from ($A1) to ($A3), then advances screen pointer by 2

$B04B.name: advance_screen_ptr
$B04B.purpose: Advances screen pointer $A3/$A4 by A bytes, also adds A to $B4 row offset accumulator. Core utility for terrain rendering

$B05C.name: start_explosion_sound
$B05C.purpose: Initiates explosion sound effect: sets frequency $B1=$90, X=$61 (POKEY channel), Y=$23 (duration). Falls through to score rendering setup

$B064.name: render_score_small
$B064.purpose: Renders 2-digit score value to display. Loads A=#$02 (2 digits) then branches to $B086

$B068.name: render_lives_display
$B068.purpose: Renders lives/status display: A=#$50 (sprite data), Y=#$0C (12 scanlines). Sets up rendering at X=$63 (page $06)

$B06E.name: render_score_dynamic
$B06E.purpose: Renders dynamically-sized score. Computes source page from sector ($09 AND $0E)*2+$80, sets Y=#$0C (12 lines), A=#$D0 (sprite page)

$B07C.name: render_score_mini
$B07C.purpose: Renders small 2-line score element: A=#$10 (16 bytes), Y=#$02 (2 rows)

$B0C7.name: write_distance_digits
$B0C7.purpose: Writes 4 distance/bridge counter digits to display list area $3F04. Reads BCD digits from table at $B0E9, writes to $3F04+$A6, increments $A6

$B0D8.name: write_score_digits
$B0D8.purpose: Writes 4 score digits to display list area $3F04. Reads from table at $B0ED, writes to $3F04+$A5, increments $A5

$B0F1.name: game_init
$B0F1.purpose: Full game initialization. Loads initial variable values from $BAE1 table into $4D-$54. Clears pages $0B-$0F and $20-$3E. Sets $C7 init flag. Clears game state variables $2B-$4C

$B183.name: prng_step
$B183.purpose: Advances the 3-byte LFSR pseudo-random number generator. Shifts $AB left 5, XORs with $AB, shifts into carry, rotates through $AA/$AC/$AB

$B194.name: advance_terrain_sector
$B194.purpose: Advances to next terrain sector. Saves current PRNG seed ($66-$69) to $AD-$B0, loads new seed from PRNG ($AA-$AC) into $66-$69. Clamps terrain index $60 to range [0,$50). Stores player X ($52) into $69

$B1C0.name: handle_sector_transition
$B1C0.purpose: Manages terrain sector boundary transitions. Saves sound params ($7A/$7B). Compares current vs previous game state ($4C vs $23) for bit 2 changes. Calls $B194 to advance or $B1DD to retreat terrain sector. Always sets $5E=#$06 (row height)

$B21A.name: reset_score_display
$B21A.purpose: Resets the score display area: sets $18=#$FF (max distance), X=#$FF, branches to $B200 for full score reset

$B221.name: clear_high_score_row
$B221.purpose: Clears a high score table row: writes A=#$00 to $0B01+X through $0B08+X. Uses $56 (lives) as index, clamped to max $1B

$B223.name: write_score_to_table
$B223.purpose: Writes current score value (in A) to high score table at $0B01-$0B08 indexed by $56. Clamps index to $1B maximum

$B247.name: swap_terrain_buffers
$B247.purpose: Double-buffer swap for terrain rendering. Tests $09 bit 0; if changed, toggles $77 flag and swaps all 11 bytes between $60-$6A and $6B-$75 (terrain state for two river banks)

$B261.name: check_and_insert_score
$B261.purpose: Compares 3-byte score at ($A1) against current high at ($A3). If greater or equal, copies score and generates checksum entries using $B298. Writes to $0090,Y

$B298.name: compute_checksum
$B298.purpose: Copy-protection checksum computation. Sums $80-$8E with running XOR against $A0. Returns accumulated value in A. Used for both boot verification and score integrity

$B480.name: vbi_deferred_exit
$B480.purpose: Deferred VBI exit stub. The instruction at $B480 is a NOP (opcode $04), followed by JMP XITVBV ($E462) to return from vertical blank interrupt

data_regions.description: Score/distance digit display data. 4+4 bytes: $B0E9-$B0EC = distance display digits (30 00 4E 0E), $B0ED-$B0F0 = score display digits (20 00 4E 0E)
VBI (Vertical Blank Interrupt) handler continuation - terrain scrolling, color register updates, and display list manipulation. Runs every frame.
VBI exit stubs and IRQ handler (disables IRQ at $B488: sta IRQEN, then RTI)
Entity parameter table. Contains terrain seed values and entity shape pointers used by $AF80 to configure each sector's sprite set. 8 groups of 4 bytes for PRNG seeds, plus size/count parameters
Entity type flags table - 12 bytes of 00/01 values indicating entity properties (shootable, moving, fuel depot, etc.)
Speed/timing table - 18 ascending values ($0C to $DE then $00) used for difficulty-scaled timing. Likely scroll speed or enemy speed per difficulty level
Terrain shape data pointers - pairs of addresses pointing into $B900+ terrain shape definitions. 7 entries: $B94C, $B91A, $B94C, $B9E9, $B9F4, $B9FF, $BA0A
Terrain width/shape parameters. First 8 bytes = terrain widths (30,40,50,60,...). Next 8 = terrain type flags (04,04,04,04,08,08,...). Last 8 = color indices (1C,1C,06,06,...)

DLI (Display List Interrupt) kernel - the main rendering engine. Runs on every scanline group. Sets player/missile positions, reads entity tables, handles collision register shadows, manages color cycling per scanline. The heart of the graphics engine.
Second DLI handler / bottom-of-screen handler at $B72A. Handles score area rendering, missile positions for bottom HUD, and VBI exit

Color and position tables for DLI kernel. Includes HPOS values for river banks, color cycling values ($B7B0+), entity color palettes ($B7C0+), terrain row heights ($B7D0+), and scroll position table ($B7E0+). Last bytes at $B7F0+ are signed direction vectors
River bank edge sprite patterns. Two 16-byte sets of 4-color mode patterns (AA/55/6A/BF patterns) for left and right riverbank edges

Text/character display data for title screen and HUD. Contains encoded text strings: 'RIVER RAID' at $B860, 'COPYRIGHT' at $B883, 'PRESS START KEY' at $B8B6, 'USE JOYSTICK' at $B8C8. Character set uses custom encoding (not ATASCII)
Terrain shape definitions - encoded river/island patterns used by terrain generator. Contains compact shape data that the rendering routines expand into screen-width terrain rows
Entity sprite graphics data - jet, helicopter, ship, bridge, fuel depot shapes. Each sprite is typically 8-12 bytes tall in 4-color PM graphics format
Game initialization values table - 13 bytes loaded into $4D-$59 at game start by $B0F1. Contains initial values for lives, fuel, player position, entity counters
Entity behavior/position lookup tables. Includes entity spawn positions ($BB10+), movement patterns, and collision box dimensions. Indexed by entity type
Extended terrain shape data - wider/more complex terrain patterns for later game sectors. Dense 2-bit-per-pixel patterns for 4-color terrain rendering
Additional terrain patterns for advanced sectors. Contains repeating AA/55 patterns suggesting alternating riverbank textures
Sprite animation frames and miscellaneous lookup data. Contains compact encoded sprite shapes for animated entities (helicopter rotor positions, ship movement, explosion frames)

system_architecture.display_method: Custom DLI kernel (no OS display handler). ANTIC mode $0E (160x4-color) with DLI at $B500 managing per-scanline entity rendering
system_architecture.vbi_handler: $B2A5 (immediate VBI) -> handles sound, then falls through to terrain/scroll update at $B400+
system_architecture.dli_handler: $B500 (VDSLST) -> main rendering kernel, entity positioning, collision shadow capture
system_architecture.irq_handler: $B485 -> disables IRQEN and returns (minimal IRQ stub)
system_architecture.terrain_generation: LFSR PRNG at $B183 seeds terrain. Sector-based: each sector uses saved PRNG state in $66-$69. Double-buffered swap at $B247
system_architecture.entity_system: 5 entity slots at $0500 (11 bytes each). Entity types stored in $42-$4B. DLI reads entity Y positions from $0C-$14 to determine scanline rendering
system_architecture.scoring: Score written to $0B01-$0B08. Display digits rendered by $B0C7/$B0D8 to $3F04+. Checksum protection via $B298
system_architecture.sound: 4 POKEY channels managed in VBI at $B2A5+. $C6 = bullet sound timer, $31 = engine sound, $24 = sound state machine
system_architecture.copy_protection: Checksum at $B298 sums $80-$8E XOR $A0. Verified at boot ($A037) and used for score integrity ($B261)
